questions.question: Which of the following describes the connection of a synarthrotic joint?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Fibrous

questions.question: The knees are _____ to the shoulders.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Distal

questions.question: Adduction is:
questions.correctAnswer: Sideways movement toward the midline of the body
wrongAnswers: Sideways movement away from the midline of the body
Bending movement that decreases the angle between parts
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Sideways movement toward the midline of the body

questions.question: Which of the following actions occurs in the midsagittal plane? (Select all that apply)
questions.correctAnswer: Forward and backward movement, Extension, Flexion
wrongAnswers: Circular movement combining flexion, extension, abduction, and adduction
Movement toward or away from the midline
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Forward and backward movement, Extension, Flexion

questions.question: Lateral refers to a structure that is:
questions.correctAnswer: Away from the midline of the body
wrongAnswers: Sideways movement toward the midline of the body
Turning a bone around its own axis
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Away from the midline of the body

questions.question: The ankles are more proximal to the hips than the knees.
questions.explanation: This statement is false.

questions.question: If I straighten my arm, this is called extension of the arm.
questions.explanation: This statement is correct.

questions.question: From anatomical position, if you raise your arms and stretch them out away from the sides of the body, then you are _______ your arms.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Abducting

questions.question: If a structure is towards the midline of the body, it is in the _____ direction.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Medial

questions.question: In regards to the three different classes of joints: ___________________ are freely movable, ______________are immovable, and ___________________ are slightly movable.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Diarthrotic, synarthrotic, amphiarthrotic

questions.question: If I bend my knee, the anatomical name for this movement is extension of the knee.
questions.explanation: This statement is false.

questions.question: The thumb joint is which type of joint?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Diarthrotic

questions.question: If I bend over to touch my toes, I am _____ at the hip joints.
wrongAnswers: Away from the midline of the body
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Flexing

questions.question: The plane that divides the body into right and left sides is the:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Midsagittal plane

questions.question: The joint at the base of the thumb is an example of what joint type?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Saddle

questions.question: The ankles are _____ to the knees.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Inferior

questions.question: Which of the following actions occurs in the coronal plane? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Sideways movement, Abduction

questions.question: A pivot joint is an example of a joint that is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Uni-axial

questions.question: Biaxial joints allow for movement in only one direction.
questions.explanation: This statement is false.

questions.question: The movement of rotation happens in the:
wrongAnswers: Sideways movement toward the midline of the body
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Transverse plane

questions.question: Which of the following is part of the axial skeleton?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The spine

questions.question: Which is an example of an ellipsoidal joint?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The wrist joint

questions.question: The action of shrugging my shoulders happens in which plane of movement?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The coronal plane

questions.question: The pubic symphysis is an example of an amphiarthrotic joint.
questions.explanation: This statement is correct.

questions.question: The pelvis is part of the appendicular skeleton.
questions.explanation: This statement is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following movements happens in the coronal plane?
wrongAnswers: Bending movement that decreases the angle between parts
Movement toward or away from the midline
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Sideways movement

questions.question: If I turn my head to the side, the movement I am doing is called:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Rotation

questions.question: The posterior side of the body is the:
wrongAnswers: Movement toward or away from the midline
Bending movement that decreases the angle between parts
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Back

questions.question: Abduction of the arm happens when I:
questions.correctAnswer: Reach my arm out to the side
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Reach my arm out to the side

questions.question: An example of a diarthrotic joint is:
wrongAnswers: Sideways movement toward the midline of the body
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: All of the above

questions.question: Anterior refers to the _____ of a structure on the body.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Front

questions.question: The action of looking over your shoulder happens in which plane of movement?
wrongAnswers: Reach my arm out to the side
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The transverse plane

questions.question: The arms are part of the axial skeleton
questions.explanation: This statement is false.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of an amphiarthrotic joint?
wrongAnswers: Reach my arm out to the side
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Intervertebral joints

questions.question: An example of synarthrotic joints are:
wrongAnswers: Movement that increases the angle of a joint
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The sutures in the skull

questions.question: Which action occurs in the sagittal plane?
questions.correctAnswer: Pressing the gas pedal to the floor
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Pressing the gas pedal to the floor

questions.question: The shoulders are _____ to the hips.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Superior

questions.question: Extension is:
questions.correctAnswer: Movement that increases the angle of a joint
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Movement that increases the angle of a joint

questions.question: Isometric muscular contractions happen when a muscle:
questions.correctAnswer: Contracts without getting shorter or longer
wrongAnswers: Muscle shortens as it overcomes resistance
Muscle contracts at a constant speed
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Contracts without getting shorter or longer

questions.question: Using the same amount of power, a muscle shortens as it overcomes resistance. What type of contraction is this?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Isotonic concentric

questions.question: A fascicle is a tube of connective tissue surrounding a group of muscle cells.
questions.explanation: This statement is correct.

questions.question: Isotonic eccentric muscular contraction happens when the muscle gets ______during contraction.
wrongAnswers: Contracts without getting shorter or longer
Muscle contracts at a constant speed
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Longer

questions.question: If we stretch our arms out to our sides, our deltoids are doing a/an __________ contraction to lift the arms.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Concentric

questions.question: The thin type of protein anchored to the z-line is called:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Actin

questions.question: When muscles contract, they move bones. The more stable bone of attachment is called the ________________, while the more movable bone is called the ________________ of that muscle.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Origin; insertion

questions.question: An example of an isotonic muscular contraction is holding a weight steadily in your hand without moving.
questions.explanation: This statement is false.

questions.question: A muscle whose contraction actually produces the movement is known as the:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Agonist

questions.question: Which of the following is not one of the functions of the muscular system?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Mineral storage

questions.question: An isometric contraction does NOT produce movements.
questions.explanation: This statement is correct.

questions.question: When you tear a muscle, what you actually tear is:
wrongAnswers: Contracts without getting shorter or longer
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Connective tissue

questions.question: A muscle cell is a row of actin and myosin molecules surrounded by a layer of:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Connective tissue

questions.question: A spastic muscle has less than normal tone.
questions.explanation: This statement is false.

questions.question: When a skeletal muscle can no longer contract despite neural stimulation, it is referred to as:
wrongAnswers: All or none of the muscle cell will contract when stimulated
Contracts without getting shorter or longer
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Muscle fatigue

questions.question: If I lower my body towards the floor from a push-up position, my triceps are contracting while also getting longer. What type of contraction is occurring in my triceps?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Isotonic eccentric

questions.question: A muscle that works in conjunction with another muscle to create a particular action is called a:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Synergist

questions.question: The functional unit of a muscle cell is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Sarcomere

questions.question: Generally, a muscle could be described as a type of connective tissue with specialized cells containing two proteins.
questions.explanation: This statement is correct.

questions.question: Muscles function to:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: All of the above

questions.question: The all-or-none principle simply states:
questions.correctAnswer: All or none of the muscle cell will contract when stimulated
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: All or none of the muscle cell will contract when stimulated

questions.question: The image shown is which muscle?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Brachialis

questions.question: In this image we see one of the bony landmarks where brachialis inserts. It is the:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The ulnar tuberosity

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Supraglenoid tubercle

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: olecranon process of the ulna

questions.question: The image shown is which muscle?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Biceps brachii

questions.question: This image shows the insertion of which muscle?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Triceps brachii

questions.question: This image shows the origin of which muscle?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Triceps brachii (long head)

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Coracoid process

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Pectoralis minor origin

questions.question: The levator scapulae does which actions?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Elevation and downward rotation

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Serratus anterior

questions.question: The image shown is which muscle?

questions.question: The image shows the origin for which muscle?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Levator scapulae

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Deltoids

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Origin of anterior deltoids

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Abduction of the humerus

questions.question: The image shows which muscle?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Teres major

questions.question: The image shows which of the following bony landmarks?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Sternum

questions.question: Which of the following muscles do medial rotation?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Subscapularis, pectoralis major, teres major

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Subscapularis

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Insertion of the deltoids

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Deltoid

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Thoracolumbar aponeurosis

questions.question: Which muscle laterally rotates the humerus?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Teres minor

questions.question: The image shows which of the following bony landmarks?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Infraspinous fossa

questions.question: Which muscle does the same actions as latissimus dorsi except for one?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Teres major

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Supraspinatus

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Inferior angle of scapula

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Pectoralis major

questions.question: Which of the following muscles is antagonistic to itself?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Deltoids

questions.question: Which muscle might be especially well-developed in someone who is a swimmer?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Latissimus dorsi

questions.question: All three hamstring muscles can create which movements?
questions.correctAnswer: Flexion of the knee and extension of the hip
wrongAnswers: Extension of the knee and flexion of the hip
Flexion of the knee and flexion of the hip
Extension of the knee and extension of the hip
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Flexion of the knee and extension of the hip

questions.question: All four of the quadriceps muscles insert together on which bony landmark?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Tibial tuberosity

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Semimembranosus

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Rectus femoris

questions.question: Which quadricep muscle can act to flex the hip joint as well as extend the knee joint?

questions.question: Which of the hamstring muscles can create lateral rotation of a flexed knee?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Biceps femoris

questions.question: Which of the following muscles does plantarflexion of the ankle and assists in flexion of the knee?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Gastrocnemius

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Calcaneus

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Head of the fibula

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Dorsiflexion and inversion

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Abduction at the hip

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Lateral rotation of the hip

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Gracilis

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Sartorius

Only the first two answers are correct
None of the answers are correct

questions.question: The gluteus medius and gluteus minimus both originate from a place on which bone?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The Ilium

questions.question: Which two muscles can do actions that are antagonistic to themselves?

questions.question: Which two muscles do lateral rotation of the femur?

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Gluteus maximus

questions.question: The five muscles: pectineus, adductor brevis, adductor longus, gracilis, and adductor magnus, all do what as their primary action?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Adduction of the hip

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: All are correct

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Adductor longus

questions.question: The image shows which muscle?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Sartorius

questions.question: Iliopsoas and gluteus maximus are synergistic (work together) in which of the following actions?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Lateral rotation

questions.question: Which muscle originates between the superior and middle gluteal lines?

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Manubrium of the sternum

Only the first two answers are correct
None of the answers are correct

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Sternocleidomastoid

questions.question: When acting unilaterally, the internal obliques do which actions?

Flex the trunk and rotate it to the same side
Extend the trunk and rotate it to the same side
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Laterally flex the trunk and rotate it to the same side

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Pubic symphysis

questions.question: When acting unilaterally, the external obliques do which actions?

wrongAnswers: Laterally flex the trunk and rotate it to the same side
Flex the trunk and rotate it to the opposite side
Extend the trunk and rotate it to the opposite side

wrongAnswers: Only the first answer is correct
Only the first two answers are correct
None of the answers are correct
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: All answers are correct

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Vertebrae L1-L3, Xiphoid process, Lower 6 costal cartilages

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Diaphragm

questions.question: The internal intercostals originate and insert:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Between adjacent ribs
